name: Chicken Satay
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Skinless, boned chicken - breast, cut into long, thin strips
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan over high heat, combine the chicken stock, cayenne pepper, heavy cream, black pepper, chopped peanuts, peanut butter, creole seasoning, sesame oil, green onions, soy sauce, minced garlic, and cilantro.
2. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3. Remove from heat. Thread the chicken onto metal skewers in the long direction, dividing it equally among the skewers (using about 16 skewers).
4. Sprinkle creole seasoning over the meat and press it into the chicken with your hands.
5. Heat a large skillet over high heat and sear the chicken until brown on each side.
6. Spoon some sauce onto individual plates, arrange the skewers on top (2 for an appetizer or 4 for a main course), and sprinkle with green onions.
New Orleans influenced version of traditional satay from "Emeril's New New Orleans Cooking" by Emeril Lagasse & Jessie Tirsch.
